Serialized Form

  • Package com.pax.poslink

    • Class com.pax.poslink.BatchRequest extends BaseRequest<BatchRequest> implements Serializable

      • Serialized Fields

        • TransType

          int TransType
          Indicate the transaction type.

          BATCHCLOSE = 1 - used to close the current batch
          FORCEBATCHCLOSE = 2 - used to force close the current batch, if host supports it.
          BATCHCLEAR = 3 - used to clear local database
          PURGEBATCH = 4 - used to purge batch
          SAFUPLOAD = 5 - This command is used to launch store and forward (SAF) transactions uploading, if supported by host.
          DELETESAFFILE = 6 - This command uses the delete store and forward (SAF) records once upload success or failed.
          DELETETRANSACTION = 7 - This command is used to delete a transaction log from database.
          An Error will be returned while invoking POSLink.ProcessTrans if user set other value.
          User can assign the TransType by com.PAX.POSLink.BatchRequest.ParseTransType or assign an integer directly.
          Example:
          BatchRequest batch = new BatchRequest();
          batch.TransType = batch.ParseTransType("BATCHCLOSE"); //recommend
          or
          batch.TransType = 1;

        • EDCType

          int EDCType
          Indicate the EDC type.

          ALL = 0
          CREDIT = 1
          DEBIT = 2
          CHECK = 3
          EBT = 4
          GIFT = 5
          LOYALTY = 6
          CASH = 7
          Only above value accepted, other value will be omitted.
          User can assign the EDCType by com.PAX.POSLink.BatchRequest.ParseEDCType or assign an integer directly.
          Example: BatchRequest batch = new BatchRequest();
          batch.EDCType = batch.ParseEDCType("CREDIT"); //recommend
          or
          batch.EDCType = 1;

        • Timestamp

          java.lang.String Timestamp
          Time/date stamp of transaction.

          Attribute: n14

          The date time, YYYYMMDDhhmmss

        • SAFIndicator

          java.lang.String SAFIndicator
          Store and forward upload type indicator

          Attribute: n1

          0: New stored transactions. 1: Failed transactions 2: All (upload/resend Failed + New records)

          Only valid by SAFUPLOAD and DELETESAFFILETransType

        • CardType

          int CardType
          Indicate the Card type.

          Attribute: n2

          VISA = 1
          MASTERCARD = 2
          AMEX = 3
          DISCOVER = 4
          DINERCLUB = 5
          ENROUTE = 6
          JCB = 7
          REVOLUTIONCARD = 8
          OTHER = 9
          Only above value accepted, other value will be omitted.
          Used only TransType is LOCALDETAILREPORT/ LOCALTOTALREPORT
          User can assign the CardType by com.PAX.POSLink.ReportRequest.ParseCardType or assign an integer directly.
          Example: BatchRequest batch = new BatchRequest();
          batch.CardType = report.ParseCardType("MASTERCARD"); //recommend
          or
          batch.CardType = 2;

          Only valid when TransType =DELETETRANSACTION

        • PaymentType

          int PaymentType
          Indicate the payment type.

          Attribute: n..2

          See PaymentTransType for definition. User can assign the PaymentType by BatchRequest.ParsePaymentType(String) or assign an integer directly.
          Example:
          BatchRequest batch = new BatchRequest();
          batch.PaymentType = batch.ParseTransType("ADJUST"); //recommend
          or
          batch.PaymentType = 10;

          Only valid when TransType =DELETETRANSACTION

        • RecordNum

          java.lang.String RecordNum
          The log index in terminal.

          Attribute: n..4

          Only valid when TransType =DELETETRANSACTION

        • RefNum

          java.lang.String RefNum
          Terminal reference number used for follow-up transactions.

          Attribute: n..4

          Only valid when TransType =DELETETRANSACTION

        • AuthCode

          java.lang.String AuthCode
          Retrieve the transaction record with the matching authorization number.

          Attribute: ans..10

          Only valid when TransType =DELETETRANSACTION

        • ECRRefNum

          java.lang.String ECRRefNum
          Retrieve a transaction record with the merchant reference number.

          Attribute: ans..16

          Only valid when TransType =DELETETRANSACTION

        • MultiMerchant

          MultiMerchant MultiMerchant
          MultiMerchant Information. see MultiMerchant for detail.

          Only valid when TransType =BATCHCLOSE

          Since:
          V1.04.00

        • ExtData

          java.lang.String ExtData

          Extended data in XML format

          Name FormatDescription
          <MM_ID> n..2

          Used to indicate the specific merchant id, valid between [0, 24].

          Valid when TransType = BATCHCLOSE. And only apply for Multi-Merchant application.
          <MM_Name> ans..6

          Used indicate the specific merchant name.

          Valid when TransType = BATCHCLOSE. And only apply for Multi-Merchant application.

    • Class com.pax.poslink.ManageRequest extends BaseRequest<ManageRequest> implements Serializable

      • Serialized Fields

        • TransType

          int TransType
          Indicate the command type.

          INIT = 1 -This command is use to initialize PAX terminal. PAX terminal will do internal test/check and init the terminal for transactions. If terminal doesn't receive this command after install, the idle menu will display the "disMsg", when terminal receives this command, the idle menu will display the "ildeMsg"
          GETVAR = 2 -This command is used to get the control variable and configure parameter in the terminal. See Configurable Parameter Definition for definition.
          SETVAR = 3 -This command is used to set the control variable and configure parameter in the terminal. See Configurable Parameter Definition for definition.
          SHOWDIALOG = 4 -Show a half screen with title and buttons. It is supported by MT30 but it is not supported by SP30.
          GETSIGNATURE = 5 -This command is used to get the signature image file in the terminal. MT30 supports it.
          SHOWMESSAGE = 6 -Terminal can display 5 line messages, if no clear message received. If terminal received the sixth line message, terminal only displayed the last 5 line messages.
          CLEARMESSAGE = 7 -Clear the message buffer on terminal, if terminal receives this command, and then receives the show message command, the terminal only displays the message getting from the show message command.
          RESET = 8 -Reset the terminal to idle state .
          UPDATEIMAGE = 9 -Used to update image to POS .(Deprecated since V1.05.01. Please use UPDATERESOURCE instead)
          DOSIGNATURE = 10 -Used to do signature with POS directly.
          DELETEIMAGE = 11 -Used to delete the POS idle image as image name.
          SHOWTHANKYOU = 12 -Used to show message center aligned which defaults to.(Deprecated since V1.11.00. Please use SHOWMESSAGECENTER instead)
          the screen as "Thanks You".
          REBOOT = 13 -Used to reboot the terminal.
          GETPINBLOCK = 14 -Used to input PIN and get PINBLOCK back.
          INPUTACCOUNT = 15 -Used to get account input.
          RESETMSR = 16 -Used to reset MSR buffer.
          INPUTTEXT = 17 -Used to get text input.
          CHECKFILE = 18 -Used to check file existence.
          AUTHORIZECARD = 19 -Used to authorize contact EMV card.
          COMPLETEONLINEEMV = 20 -Used to complete the online authorized EMV transaction.
          REMOVECARD = 21 -Used to prompt cardholder to remove card.
          GETEMVTLVDATA = 22 -Used to get EMV TLV data from terminal.
          SETEMVTLVDATA = 23 -Used to set EMV TLV data from terminal.
          INPUTACCOUNTWITHEMV = 24 -Used to get account input with EMV card support.
          COMPLETECONTACTLESSEMV = 25 -Used to complete the contactless EMV transaction if there's any issuer authentication data or issuer script contained in the host authorization response message.(deprecated since V1.02.00)
          SETSAFPARAMETERS = 26 - This command is used for setting the store and forward (SAF) related parameters.
          SHOWTEXTBOX = 27 - This command is used for showing a text box on terminal.
          REPRINT=28 - Used to print the last receipt or any receipt.
          PRINTER=29 - Used to print the receipt refer to the data generated by POS system.
          SHOWITEM =30 – Used to display itemized product info, Similar logic as the SHOWMESSAGE.
          CARDINSERTDETECTION = 31 – Used to check card whether be inserted in terminal card slot An Error will be returned while invoking POSLink.ProcessTrans if user set other value.
          TOKENADMINISTRATIVE = 32 - It is used to do token administration with depending on hosts.
          SHOWDIALOGFORM = 33 – Show a dialog form to allow user to do single or multiple selection.
          CAMERASCAN = 34 – Use camera to scan barcode and QRCode
          VASSETMERCHANTPARAMETERS = 35 - This command is used to configure the parameters of Apple VAS or Google SmartTap.
          VASPUSHDATA = 36 - This command is used to configure the parameters of Google SmartTap to push data from terminal to mobile, such as merchant info.
          MIFARECARD = 37 - M1 Card
          GETSAFPARAMETERS = 38 - GetSAFParameter
          UPLOADFTP = 39 - Upload FTP
          SESSIONKEYINJECTION = 40 - Session Key Injection
          MACCALCULATION = 41 - MAC Calculation
          GETPEDINFORMATION = 42 - Get PED Information
          UPDATERESOURCE = 43 -Used to update resource, detail content depends on ManageRequest.FileType .
          INCREASEKSN = 44 - This command is used to get increase KSN.
          SHOWMESSAGECENTER = 45 -Used to show message center aligned which defaults to.
          CUSTOMDATAENCRYPTION = 46 -This command is used to encrypt custom data.
          User can assign the TransType by com.PAX.POSLink.ManageRequest.ParseTransType or assign an integer directly.
          Example:
          ManageRequest manage = new ManageRequest();
          manage.TransType = manage.ParseTransType("GETVAR"); //recommend
          or
          manage.TransType = 1;

        • EDCType

          int EDCType
          Indicate the EDC type.

          ALL = 0
          CREDIT = 1
          DEBIT = 2
          CHECK = 3
          EBT = 4
          GIFT = 5
          LOYALTY = 6
          CASH = 7
          Only above value accepted, other value will be omitted.
          EBT includes EBT_FOODSTAMP and EBT_CASHBENEFIT.
          User can assign the EDCType by com.PAX.POSLink.ManageRequest.ParseEDCType or assign an integer directly.
          Example: ManageRequest manage = new ManageRequest();
          manage.EDCType = manage.ParseEDCType("CREDIT"); //recommend
          or
          manage.EDCType = 1;

        • Trans

          int Trans
          Indicate the payment type.

          See PaymentTransType for definition. Only above value accepted, other value will be omitted.
          User can assign the Trans by ManageRequest.ParseTrans(String) or assign an integer directly.
          Example:
          ManageRequest manage = new ManageRequest();
          manage.Trans = manage.ParseTrans("ADJUST"); //recommend
          or
          manage.Trans = 10;

        • Title

          java.lang.String Title
          For SHOWDIALOG/GETPINBLOCK/INPUTTEXT/COMPLETEONLINEEMV/SHOWTEXTBOX:

          The display title on terminal.

          Attribute: ans…32

          For SHOWDIALOGFORM:

          Lines of text, (use all available space)

          Attribute: ans…64

          Show screen with title and buttons or menu items.

          Only valid while transType is SHOWDIALOG/GETPINBLOCK/INPUTTEXT/COMPLETEONLINEEMV/SHOWTEXTBOX/SHOWDIALOGFORM.

        • Button1

          java.lang.String Button1
          The name of button1 on terminal. Buttons 2, 3 and 4 will be ignored if this is empty.

          Attribute: ans…24

          Only valid while transType is SHOWDIALOG & SHOWTEXTBOX

          Only valid while transType is SHOWDIALOG & SHOWTEXTBOX

          java.lang.String Button3
          The name of button3 on terminal

          Attribute: ans…24

          Only valid while transType is SHOWDIALOG & SHOWTEXTBOX

          java.lang.String Button4
          The name of button4 on terminal

          Attribute: ans…24

          Only valid while transType is SHOWDIALOG

          java.lang.String DisplayMessage
          The display message on terminal

          See CONTROL-CHARACTER-FOR-TEXT-SHOWING for supported special characters.

          Only valid while transType is SHOWMESSAGE

        • DisplayMessage2

          java.lang.String DisplayMessage2
          The second display message on terminal. Conditional for models with different screen size.

          Attribute: ans…36

          See CONTROL-CHARACTER-FOR-TEXT-SHOWING for supported special characters.

          Valid only when the TransType = SHOWMESSAGE.

        • X

          java.lang.String X
          Deprecated. 
          The X axis position of the upper-left corner of the region

          Only valid while transType is CLEARSCREEN

        • Y

          java.lang.String Y
          Deprecated. 
          Y axis position of the upper-left corner of the region

          Only valid while transType is CLEARSCREEN

        • W

          java.lang.String W
          Deprecated. 
          the pixels width of the region to be cleared

          Only valid while transType is CLEARSCREEN

        • H

          java.lang.String H
          Deprecated. 
          the pixels height of the region to be cleared

          Only valid while transType is CLEARSCREEN

        • ImagePath

          java.lang.String ImagePath
          Deprecated. use ManageRequest.FilePath instead.
          The path of ".zip" file, the "zip" including idle images must be BMP

          Attribute: var

          format and the suffix must be ".r" .

          MT30 suggested image size is 480*272(width*height in pixels) and format should be 24bit bmp.
          S300 suggested image size is 240*248 and format should be 24bit bmp.
          S500 suggested image size is 320*200 and format should be 24bit bmp.
          S920 suggested image size is 240*280 and format should be png.
          SP30 suggested image size is 320*200 and format should be 24bit bmp.
          PX5/PX7 suggested image size is 800*440 and format should be png.
          A30 suggested image size is 1280*720 and format should be png.
          A60 suggested image size is 720*1280 and format should be png.
          A6650 suggested image size is 1080*2408 and format should be png.
          A77 suggested image size is 720*1440 and format should be png.
          A80 suggested image size is 480*800 and format should be png.
          A910S suggested image size is 720*1280 and format should be png.
          A920 suggested image size is 720*1280 and format should be png.
          A920 Pro suggested image size is 720*1440 and format should be png.
          A920 MAX suggested image size is 720*1480 and format should be png.
          A930 suggested image size is 720*1280 and format should be png.
          Aries6 suggested image size is 1280*720 and format should be png.
          Aries8 suggested image size is 800*1280 and format should be png.
          D190 suggested image size is 320*216 and format should be png.
          D210/D200/SP30s suggested image size is 320*200 and format should be 24bit bmp.
          D220 suggested image size is 480*760 and format should be png.
          IM20 suggested image size is 480*272 and format should be png.
          IM30 suggested image size is 720*1280 and format should be png.
          IM300 suggested image size is 320*216 and format should be png.
          Q20 suggested image size is 480*272 and format should be png.
          Q25 suggested image size is 320*216 and format should be png.
          Q30 suggested image size is 800*440 and format should be png.
          Q80 suggested image size is 480*726 and format should be png.
          POS expands image if size is below suggested size.

          note:
          1. The Prolin idle image file’s name must be “mt30_ad0.r” /”mt30_ad1.r”/”mt30_ad2.r”.
          2. The Android idle image file’s name must be “mt30_ad0.png” /”mt30_ad1.png”/”mt30_ad2.png” (Except the extern Android device).
          3. The extern Android device idle image file's name must be "exdev_idle.png".

          Only valid while transType is UPDATEIMAGE/UPDATERESOURCE

        • ImageName

          java.lang.String ImageName
          the name of image must be mt30_ad0/mt30_ad1/mt30_ad2

          Attribute: ans..16

          Product image name. The image is preloaded on the terminal. Conditional for models with different screen size and terminal capability.

          Only valid while transType is DELETEIMAGE

        • Upload

          int Upload
          the upload flag, the value is as below:

          1: need upload to host
          0: do not need upload to host. The default value is 0
          Only valid while transType is DOSIGNATURE

          Attribute: n1

        • HRefNum

          java.lang.String HRefNum
          The host reference number

          Attribute: ans…32

          Only valid while transType is DOSIGNATURE

        • TimeOut

          java.lang.String TimeOut
          Timeout for DOSIGNATURE/ GETPINBLOCK / INPUTACCOUNT/INPUTTEXT/ AUTHORIZECARD/ INPUTACCOUNTWITHEMV /SHOWTEXTBOX/SHOWDIALOG/SHOWDIALOGFORM/UPDATERESOURCE/UPDATEIMG in 100ms increments.

          Attribute: n...4

          For DOSIGNATURE, The timeout unit is 100ms. The valid value should be from 150 to 9999. If value is null, it means no timeout and waiting for user cancelation or confirmation.

          For GETPINBLOCK/AUTHORIZECARD, Valid value should be [0, 3000]. The value of “” or 0 means waiting till user canceled or confirmed.

          For INPUTACCOUNT or INPUTACCOUNTWITHEMV, valid value should be [200, 9999].

          For INPUTTEXT or SHOWDIALOGFORM. valid value should be [1, 9999]. The value of "" means waiting till user canceled or confirmed.

          For CAMERASCAN, should be [50, 9999].

          For SHOWDIALOG or SHOWTEXTBOX, Valid value should be [0, 9999]. The value of “” means waiting till user canceled or confirmed. The value of “0” means non-blocking mode, only valid when no button.

          For UPDATERESOURCE or UPDATEIMG, Valid value should be [200, 9999]. The default value is 1200. When file upload finished, terminal need to execute installation before returning the final result. The value specifies the time POSLink need to wait till terminal install finished. If the value exceeds the scope or is empty, we will use default value(1200)

        • ThankYouTitle

          java.lang.String ThankYouTitle
          The title of thank you screen.

          Attribute: var

          See CONTROL-CHARACTER-FOR-TEXT-SHOWING for supported special characters.

          Only valid while transType is SHOWTHANKYOU/SHOWMESSAGECENTER

        • ThankYouMessage1

          java.lang.String ThankYouMessage1
          The message1 of thank you screen.

          Attribute: var

          See CONTROL-CHARACTER-FOR-TEXT-SHOWING for supported special characters.

          Only valid while transType is SHOWTHANKYOU/SHOWMESSAGECENTER

        • ThankYouMessage2

          java.lang.String ThankYouMessage2
          The message2 of thank you screen.

          Attribute: var

          See CONTROL-CHARACTER-FOR-TEXT-SHOWING for supported special characters.

          Only valid while transType is SHOWTHANKYOU/SHOWMESSAGECENTER

        • ThankYouTimeOut

          java.lang.String ThankYouTimeOut
          Timeout in 100ms. Valid value should be [0, 9999].
          The value of “0” means non-blocking mode.

          Attribute: n...3

          Only valid while transType is SHOWTHANKYOU/SHOWMESSAGECENTER

        • AccountNumber

          java.lang.String AccountNumber
          The account number must have a length between 13 and 19 characters.
          It's mandatory when the 'PIN Algorithm' is set to 0 (ISO9564_0) or 3 (ISO9564 3), with Encryption Type as 1 (DES DUKPT) or 2 (Master Session DES).
          Additionally, it's required for 4 (ISO9564_4) when the Encryption Type is 3 (AES DUKPT). When encryption is active, this value should be the masked PAN from the INPUTACCOUNTWITHEMV response.

          Attribute: n ..19

          Valid only when TransType = GETPINBLOCK.

        • EncryptionType

          java.lang.String EncryptionType
          The encryption type for PINBLOCK calculation. Default as 1.

          Attribute: n1

          The value is as below:
          1: DUKPT.(DES DUKPT)
          2: Master Session DES
          3: AES DUKPT.
          Valid for GETPINBLOCK/AUTHORIZECARD

        • KeySlot

          java.lang.String KeySlot
          The Key Slot.

          For GETPEDINFORMATION:
          Slot, Slots or Range of Slot; Use Comma (,) to separate slot numbers and Dash ( - ) to indicate range
          Empty value means all slot.

          Attribute: var

          For GETPINBLOCK/INPUTACCOUNT/AUTHORIZECARD/INCREASEKSN:
          Data Encryption key slot number, start from 1.

          Pax Platform Pax Models Valid value
          MonitorS80,S300,S500,SP30,D210. 1-40(DES DUKPT and Master Session)
          Prolin D200,D220, S920,Q20,Q30,Px5,Px7. 1-99 (DES DUKPT and Master Session) 1-40(AES DUKPT)
          PayDroid A60, A80, A920, A930, Aries Series. 1-99 (DES DUKPT and Master Session) 1-40(AES DUKPT)

          Attribute: n..2

          For INPUTACCOUNTWITHEMV:
          For Encryption Flag as 1, 2, 5, 6:

          Pax Platform Pax Models Valid value
          MonitorS80,S300,S500,SP30,D210. 1-40(DES DUKPT and Master Session)
          Prolin D200,D220, S920,Q20,Q30,Px5,Px7. 1-99 (DES DUKPT and Master Session) 1-40(AES DUKPT)
          PayDroid A60, A80, A920, A930, Aries Series. 1-99 (DES DUKPT and Master Session) 1-40(AES DUKPT)
          For Encryption Flag as 3, valid value is 2 – 89.

          Attribute: n..2

          Required for GETPINBLOCK/INPUTACCOUNT/AUTHORIZECARD/GETPEDINFORMATION/INPUTACCOUNTWITHEMV/INCREASEKSN
          Note: if it's an AUTHORIZECARD request, it will be effective only when the CVM type ONLINE PIN is applied.

        • PinMinLength

          java.lang.String PinMinLength
          MIN length for PIN entry. Default as 4.

          Attribute: n..2

          Valid for GETPINBLOCK/AUTHORIZECARD

        • PinMaxLength

          java.lang.String PinMaxLength
          MAX length for PIN entry. Default as 12

          Attribute: n..2

          Valid for GETPINBLOCK/AUTHORIZECARD

        • NullPinFlag

          java.lang.String NullPinFlag
          Deprecated. 
          Deprecated since V1.02.00. If it is GETPINBLOCK please use ManageRequest.NullPin, if it is AUTHORIZECARD please use ManageRequest.PINBypass.

          Flag indicates whether NULL PIN allowed during PIN entry.

          Default as 0. Valid values:

          0 - Not allowed, 1 - Allowed.
          Valid for GETPINBLOCK/AUTHORIZECARD

        • NullPin

          java.lang.String NullPin
          This field is effective only when PIN is required. Allow user to bypass the PIN.

          Valid values:
          0: Do not allow.
          1: Allowed.
          Default = 0.
          Valid only when TransType = GETPINBLOCK

        • PINBypass

          java.lang.String PINBypass
          This field is effective only when PIN is required.

          Allow user to bypass the PIN.
          The value is as below, default to 0:
          0: not allow PIN Bypass
          1: bypass offline PIN automatically
          2: bypass online PIN automatically
          3: bypass both offline and online PINs automatically.
          4: allow user to bypass both offline and online PINs
          Valid only when TransType = AUTHORIZECARD

        • PinAlgorithm

          java.lang.String PinAlgorithm
          When the Encryption Type is 1(DES DUKPT) or 2(Master Session DES):
          The value is as below, default to 0:
          0: ISO9564_0
          1: ISO9564_1
          3: ISO9564_3
          When the Encryption Type is 3(AES DUKPT):
          The value is as below, default to 4:
          4: ISO9564_4
          Valid for GETPINBLOCK/AUTHORIZECARD

        • MagneticSwipeEntryFlag

          java.lang.String MagneticSwipeEntryFlag
          Flag indicates whether magnetic card swipe is allowed.

          Default as 0. Valid values:
          0 - Not allowed,
          1 - Internal Pinpad.
          2 - External Pinpad (A80 only, if use other device will return param error).
          3 - External Pinpad first. If external pinpad is present, use external pinpad. If not, use internal pinpad. (A80 only, if use other device will return param error) Valid for INPUTACCOUNT/INPUTACCOUNTWITHEMV

        • ManualEntryFlag

          java.lang.String ManualEntryFlag
          Flag indicates whether manual entry is allowed.

          Default as 0. Valid values:
          0 - Not allowed, 1 - Allowed.
          Valid for INPUTACCOUNT/INPUTACCOUNTWITHEMV

        • ContactlessEntryFlag

          java.lang.String ContactlessEntryFlag
          Flag indicates whether contactless entry is allowed.

          Default as 0. Valid values:
          0 - Not allowed,
          1 - Internal Pinpad.
          2 - External Pinpad (A80 only, if use other device will return param error).
          3 - External Pinpad first. If external pinpad is present, use external pinpad. If not, use internal pinpad. (A80 only, if use other device will return param error) Valid for INPUTACCOUNT/INPUTACCOUNTWITHEMV

        • ContactEMVEntryFlag

          java.lang.String ContactEMVEntryFlag
          Flag indicates whether contact EMV entry is allowed.

          Default as 0. Valid values:
          0 - Not allowed,
          1 - Internal Pinpad.
          2 - External Pinpad (A80 only, if use other device will return param error).
          3 - External Pinpad first. If external pinpad is present, use external pinpad. If not, use internal pinpad. (A80 only, if use other device will return param error) Valid for INPUTACCOUNTWITHEMV

        • ScannerEntryFlag

          java.lang.String ScannerEntryFlag
          Flag indicates whether scanner entry is allowed.

          Attribute: n1

          Default as 0. Valid values:
          0 - Not allowed, 1 - Allowed.
          Valid for INPUTACCOUNT/INPUTACCOUNTWITHEMV

        • FrontCameraFlag

          java.lang.String FrontCameraFlag
          Not supported now

        • RearCameraFlag

          java.lang.String RearCameraFlag
          Not supported now

        • ExpiryDatePrompt

          java.lang.String ExpiryDatePrompt
          Flag indicates whether expiry date needs to be prompted during manual * entry.

          Attribute: n1

          Default as 0. Valid values:
          0: Do not prompt.
          1: Prompt: Bypass Allowed
          2: Prompt: Bypass Not Allowed. Valid for INPUTACCOUNT/INPUTACCOUNTWITHEMV

        • EncryptionFlag

          java.lang.String EncryptionFlag
          Encryption Method for captured account data.

          Attribute: n1

          Default as 0. Valid values:
          0: not encrypted.
          1: DUKPT DES with PIN method.
          2: DES/TDES method (depends on the key length: DES for 8/16 bytes key, TDES for 24 bytes key). 3: Voltage E2EE.

          For INPUTACCOUNT:
          0: not encrypted.
          1: DUKPT DES with PIN method.
          2: DES/TDES method(Depends on the Key length: TDES for 24 bytes key DES for 8/16 bytes key).
          3: Voltage E2EE.
          The application will convert it from hexadecimal binary array into an ASCII string. For example, the 3 bytes binary array “\x1a\x23\xd4” can be converted into “1a23d4”.

          For INPUTACCOUNTWITHEMV:
          0: not encrypted.
          1: DUKPT Triple DES Key with CBC mode.
          2: DUKPT Triple DES Key with ECB mode.
          3: DES/TDES method (Depends on the Key length: TDES for 24 bytes key, DES for 8/16 bytes key).
          4: Voltage E2EE.
          5: AES-128 CBC DUKPT.
          6: AES-128 ECB DUKPT.
          7: AES-192 CBC DUKPT.
          8: AES-192 ECB DUKPT.
          9: AES-256 CBC DUKPT.
          10: AES-256 ECB DUKPT.
          The application will convert it from hexadecimal binary array into an ASCII string. For example, the 3 bytes binary array 0x1a 0x23 0xd4 can be converted into "1a23d4".

          Valid for INPUTACCOUNT/INPUTACCOUNTWITHEMV

        • MINAccountLength

          java.lang.String MINAccountLength
          MIN length for account. Default as 10. Valid value [1, 32].

          Attribute: n..2

          Valid for INPUTACCOUNT/INPUTACCOUNTWITHEMV

        • MAXAccountLength

          java.lang.String MAXAccountLength
          MAX length for account. Default as 19. Valid value [1, 32].

          Attribute: n..2

          Valid for INPUTACCOUNT/INPUTACCOUNTWITHEMV

        • InputType

          java.lang.String InputType
          Default Input Value with max length of 32.

          Attribute: n1

          Default as 0. Valid values:
          0 - Alpha-Numeric string.
          1 - Numeric string.
          2 - date in MMDDYYYY format.
          3 - time in HHMMSS format.
          4 - Currency in XXXXX.XX format.
          5 - Password format.
          6 - for phone number as “(xxx) xxx-xxxx”
          7 - for social security as “xxx-xx-xxxx”
          Valid for INPUTTEXT/SHOWTEXTBOX.

        • DefaultValue

          java.lang.String DefaultValue
          Default Input Value with max length of 32.

          Attribute: ans…32

          Valid for INPUTTEXT

        • MINLength

          java.lang.String MINLength
          MIN length of input text. Default as 0. Valid value [0, 32].

          Attribute: n..2

          Valid for INPUTTEXT/SHOWTEXTBOX

        • MAXLength

          java.lang.String MAXLength
          MAX length of input text. Default as 32. Valid value [1, 32].

          Attribute: n..2

          Valid for INPUTTEXT/SHOWTEXTBOX

        • FileName

          java.lang.String FileName
          The file name with the suffix. Valid length [1, 16].

          Attribute: ans..16

          Valid for CHECKFILE

        • SigSavePath

          java.lang.String SigSavePath
          The signature file path.

          Attribute: ans128

          If this is not set, it will use the AbstractPosLink.appDataFolder + "/img/receipt/" to save the signature

          Valid for GETSIGNATURE

        • Amount

          java.lang.String Amount
          The base amount, the format is $$$$$$$CC except LOYALTY, if the transaction is LOYALTY, this field is the points, if this field is NULL, terminal will prompt dialog to enter the amount.

          If the terminal is under demo mode, running transactions with special amount will trigger special results.
          Please refer to Special Amount in Demo Mode for more details.

          Attribute: n…9

          Valid for AUTHORIZECARD or INPUTACCOUNTWITHEMV

        • CashBackAmt

          java.lang.String CashBackAmt
          Cash back amount, $$$$$$$CC, if field is NULL, and terminal switch is on, terminal prompts dialog to enter it.

          Attribute: n…9

          Valid for AUTHORIZECARD or INPUTACCOUNTWITHEMV

        • SurchargeAmt

          java.lang.String SurchargeAmt
          Merchant surcharge fee for DEBIT/EBT, $$$$$$$CC.

          Attribute: n…9

          If the setting in terminal of "merchant fee" is 0, this field must be NULL or 0.
          If the setting in terminal of "merchant fee" is not 0, this field can be exist or NULL, if the value is NULL, terminal will use the default value in terminal.
          Only debit sale and ebt cash benefit sale support it.
          Valid for AUTHORIZECARD or INPUTACCOUNTWITHEMV

        • TipAmt

          java.lang.String TipAmt
          Tip amount $$$$$$$CC.

          Attribute: n…9

          Valid for AUTHORIZECARD or INPUTACCOUNTWITHEMV

        • TaxAmt

          java.lang.String TaxAmt
          Tax amount, $$$$$$$CC, if field is NULL, and terminal supports commercial card, terminal prompts dialog to enter it. It is included in transaction amount.

          Attribute: n…9

          Valid for AUTHORIZECARD or INPUTACCOUNTWITHEMV

        • CurrencyCode

          java.lang.String CurrencyCode
          5F2A, Transaction currency code. Default value is US dollar “840”

          Attribute: n3

          Example:
          currency code of USD is "840"
          Valid for AUTHORIZECARD or INPUTACCOUNTWITHEMV

        • CountryCode

          java.lang.String CountryCode
          Transaction country code. The default value is US "0840".

          Attribute: n4

          Example:
          country code of US is "0840"
          Valid for AUTHORIZECARD

        • MerchantDecision

          java.lang.String MerchantDecision
          Merchant decision to the current transaction. The default value is 0.

          Attribute: n1

          0: default (no merchant decision)
          1: force online
          2: force decline
          3: force acceptance
          Valid for AUTHORIZECARD

        • ExtData

          java.lang.String ExtData
          Extended data in XML format

          Example:<TagName>x</TagName>

          Note: refer to XML restriction, the below characters need to escape at first to avoid exception. ‘, “, <, >, &. Should escape as these &quot;, &apos;, &lt;, &gt;, &amp;.
          <CardTypeBitmap> n32

          The card type bitmap, refer to Card Type Bitmap definition. It makes POS system available to control which card types accepted for current command.

          Valid when TransType = INPUTACCOUNT.

          Deprecated since V1.09.01.
          <AmountLine> ans..64

          The Amount information between the TenderType TransType line and “Please Insert/Swipe Card” line on the Screen.

          Valid when TransType = INPUTACCOUNT

          Deprecated since V1.09.01.
          <MM_ID> n..2

          Used to indicate the specific merchant id, valid between [0, 24].

          Valid when TransType = SETVAR or GETVAR. And only apply for Multi-Merchant application.
          <MM_Name> ans..6

          Used indicate the specific merchant name.

          Valid when TransType = SETVAR or GETVAR. And only apply for Multi-Merchant application.

        • OnlineAuthorizationResult

          java.lang.String OnlineAuthorizationResult
          Indicate the result of online authorization.

          Attribute: n1

          0: transaction approved online
          1: transaction declined online
          2: connect host failed
          Valid for COMPLETEONLINEEMV

        • ResponseCode

          java.lang.String ResponseCode
          Host authorization response code.

          Attribute: ans2

          Host authorization response code, tag 8A.
          i.e. Contact tag 8A:
          Y1: Offline approved
          Y3: Unable to go online, offline approved
          Z1: Offline declined
          Z3: Unable to go online, offline declined
          Other: Online approved or declined
          Valid for COMPLETEONLINEEMV

        • AuthorizationCode

          java.lang.String AuthorizationCode
          EMV tag 89. Value generated by the authorization authority for an approved transaction.

          Attribute: ans6

          Valid for COMPLETEONLINEEMV

        • IssuerAuthenticationData

          java.lang.String IssuerAuthenticationData
          If issuer authentication data (tag 91) is returned by host, it shall be set to terminal.

          Attribute: ans…32

          Valid for COMPLETEONLINEEMV

        • IssuerScript1

          java.lang.String IssuerScript1
          If issuer script (tag 71) is returned by host, it shall be set to terminal.

          Attribute: ans…1024

          Valid for COMPLETEONLINEEMV

        • IssuerScript2

          java.lang.String IssuerScript2
          If issuer script (tag 72) is returned by host, it shall be set to terminal.

          Attribute: ans…1024

          Valid for COMPLETEONLINEEMV

        • Message1

          java.lang.String Message1
          the first line of message displayed on screen.

          Attribute: ans…64

          See CONTROL-CHARACTER-FOR-TEXT-SHOWING for supported special characters.

          Valid for REMOVECARD

        • Message2

          java.lang.String Message2
          the second line of message displayed on screen.

          Attribute: ans…64

          See CONTROL-CHARACTER-FOR-TEXT-SHOWING for supported special characters.

          Valid for REMOVECARD

        • TLVType

          java.lang.String TLVType
          EMV TLV type.

          Attribute: ans 2

          00 for EMV contact
          01 for EMV contactless
          Valid for GETEMVTLVDATA/SETEMVTLVDATA

        • TagList

          java.lang.String TagList
          List of requested tags. All the tags are consecutive without any separator.

          Attribute: ans…1024

          Example:
          Request for tag 0x9F26 0x9F27 0x4F, then this field would be: "9F269F274F"
          Valid for GETEMVTLVDATA

        • EMVData

          java.lang.String EMVData
          EMV data.

          Attribute: ans…1024

          If contact EMV card is read successfully, some related EMV data will be returned in consecutive TLV format data.
          Format: tag + data
          Note: All the EMV TLV data shall be expanded to double length.
          Example:
          AID (A0 00 00 00 04 10 10)"\x4F\x07\xA0\x00\x00\x00\x04\x10\x10" -> "4F07A0000000041010" Cardholder Name ("JACK")"\x5F\x20\x04\x4A\x41\x43\x4B" -> "5F20044A41434B" Valid for SETEMVTLVDATA

        • SAFMode

          java.lang.String SAFMode
          Store and forward mode indicator.

          Attribute: n1

          0: Stay Online.
          1: Stay Offline
          2: Offline Till batch.
          3: On Demand/Auto.
          Only valid while transType is SETSAFPARAMETERS

        • StartDateTime

          java.lang.String StartDateTime
          The effective start date time, YYYYMMDDhhmmss. Valid for SAF mode as 1.

          Attribute: n14

          Only valid while transType is SETSAFPARAMETERS

        • EndDateTime

          java.lang.String EndDateTime
          The effective end date time, YYYYMMDDhhmmss. Valid for SAF mode as 1.

          Attribute: n14

          Only valid while transType is SETSAFPARAMETERS

        • DurationInDays

          java.lang.String DurationInDays
          SAF On for next number of days from today Valid for SAF mode as 1.

          Attribute: n...3

          Only valid while transType is SETSAFPARAMETERS

        • MaxNumber

          java.lang.String MaxNumber
          SAF max number of record - SAF only support this number of records. i.e. 50 SAF only, once it reached it will stop working. 9999 by default.

          Attribute: n...4

          Only valid while transType is SETSAFPARAMETERS

        • TotalCeilingAmount

          java.lang.String TotalCeilingAmount
          Total ceiling amount. Once this amount is reached it will not accept any SAF record.

          Attribute: n...12

          Only valid while transType is SETSAFPARAMETERS

        • CeilingAmountPerCardType

          java.lang.String CeilingAmountPerCardType
          Once this amount is reached it will not accept any SAF record.
          Separated by Card Type with space in the card type sequence of “Visa MasterCard AMEX Diners Discover JCB enRoute VisaFleet MasterCardFleet FleetOne Fleetwide Fuleman Gascard Voyager WrightExpress Extended(others) Interac CUP Maestro Sinclair”, i.e. “2000 2000 2000 2000 2000 2000 2000 2000 2000 2000 2000 2000 2000 2000 2000 2000 2000 2000 2000 2000”.
          Note: The value of “2000” without any spaces means taking 2000 as unified ceiling amount for each card type.
          Default as empty which means ceiling amount validation will not be started during SAF.

          Attribute: ans…64

          Only valid while transType is SETSAFPARAMETERS

        • HALOPerCardType

          java.lang.String HALOPerCardType
          Once this amount per trans is reached it will not accept any SAF record.
          Separated by Card Type with space in the card type sequence of “Visa MasterCard AMEX Diners Discover JCB enRoute VisaFleet MasterCardFleet FleetOne Fleetwide Fuleman Gascard Voyager WrightExpress Extended(others) Interac CUP Maestro Sinclair”, i.e. “2000 2000 2000 2000 2000 2000 2000 2000 2000 2000 2000 2000 2000 2000 2000 2000 2000 2000 2000 2000”.
          1 exception that value of “2000” without any spaces means taking 2000 as unified HALO amount for each card type.
          Default as empty which means HALO amount validation will not be started during SAF.

          Attribute: ans…64

          Only valid while transType is SETSAFPARAMETERS

        • SAFUploadMode

          java.lang.String SAFUploadMode
          SAF Uploading mode. 0: Upload before batch. 1: Silent Auto Upload.

          Attribute: n1

          Only valid while transType is SETSAFPARAMETERS

        • AutoUploadIntervalTime

          java.lang.String AutoUploadIntervalTime
          Auto uploading checking interval in unit of 100ms.

          Attribute: n…4

          Only valid while transType is SETSAFPARAMETERS

        • DeleteSAFConfirmation

          java.lang.String DeleteSAFConfirmation
          Delete SAF Confirmation. Default as 0. 0: with Prompt. 1: without Prompt.

          Attribute: n1

          Only valid while transType is SETSAFPARAMETERS

        • ButtonColor1

          java.lang.String ButtonColor1
          The color of button1 on terminal.Please use the hexadecimal to represent the color. For example, ffffff which represent the white color. 00ff00 which means the green color. If this value not to be set, the default value will be used.

          Attribute: ans6

          Only valid while transType is SHOWTEXTBOX

        • ButtonColor2

          java.lang.String ButtonColor2
          The color of button2 on terminal.Please use the hexadecimal to represent the color. For example, ffffff which represent the white color. 00ff00 which means the green color. If this value not to be set, the default value will be used.

          Attribute: ans6

          Only valid while transType is SHOWTEXTBOX

        • ButtonColor3

          java.lang.String ButtonColor3
          The color of button3 on terminal.Please use the hexadecimal to represent the color. For example, ffffff which represent the white color. 00ff00 which means the green color. If this value not to be set, the default value will be used.

          Attribute: ans6

          Only valid while transType is SHOWTEXTBOX

        • Text

          java.lang.String Text
          The text to be displayed. Limitation depends on terminal screen size and font size, generally as many as 10 pages can be displayed on terminal.

          Attribute: ans..3072

          See CONTROL-CHARACTER-FOR-TEXT-SHOWING for supported special characters.

          Only valid while transType is SHOWTEXTBOX.

        • ButtonKey1

          java.lang.String ButtonKey1
          The hard key to be mapped for Button 1. Refer to Hard Keys Definition in API GUIDE

          Attribute: ans..24

          Only valid while transType is SHOWTEXTBOX

        • ButtonKey2

          java.lang.String ButtonKey2
          The hard key to be mapped for Button 2. Refer to Hard Keys Definition in API GUIDE

          Attribute: ans..24

          Only valid while transType is SHOWTEXTBOX

        • ButtonKey3

          java.lang.String ButtonKey3
          The hard key to be mapped for Button 3. Refer to Hard Keys Definition in API GUIDE

          Attribute: ans..24

          Only valid while transType is SHOWTEXTBOX

        • EnableHardKey

          java.lang.String EnableHardKey
          Whether to disable virtual button displaying.

          Attribute: n1

          1 - Enable Hard Key only.
          0 - Enable both virtual button and hard keys.
          0 as Default.
          Only valid while transType is SHOWTEXTBOX

        • HardKeyList

          java.lang.String HardKeyList
          Hard keys separated by , like "KEY0 KEY1". See HARD-KEYS-DEFINITION for definition. All hard keys are enabled if this field is blank from POS.

          Attribute: ans..256

          Only valid while transType is SHOWTEXTBOX

        • LastReceipt

          java.lang.String LastReceipt
          Whether to print the last receipt indicator. 1 to print.

          Attribute: n1

          Only valid when TransType = REPRINT.

        • RefNum

          java.lang.String RefNum
          Retrieve the transaction record with the matching transaction number.

          Attribute: n..4

          0 < Index <= 5000, normal data base.
          5000 < Index <= 9000, stored data base for SAF.
          9000 < Index <= 9999, failed data base for SAF.
          Only valid when TransType = REPRINT.

        • ECRRefNum

          java.lang.String ECRRefNum
          Retrieve the transaction record with the ECR reference number.

          Attribute: ans..16

          Only valid when TransType = REPRINT.

        • PrintCopy

          java.lang.String PrintCopy
          Attribute: n1
          Indicate how many receipts want to print.Default is 1.
          Only valid when TransType = PRINTER.

        • PrintData

          java.lang.String PrintData
          Variable length up to 4000.

          Attribute: ans..4000

          See PRINTDATA-DATA-FORMAT-FOR-PRINTER for definition. The ASCII characters which from 0x40 to 0x7F can be printable. Only valid when TransType = PRINTER.

        • TopDown

          java.lang.String TopDown
          Text pushed in top down or bottom up.

          Attribute: ans..1

          Y: top down
          N: bottom up.
          Default as Y, and the value is only effective at the initial request.

          Valid only when the TransType = SHOWMESSAGE or SHOWITEM.

        • TaxLine

          java.lang.String TaxLine
          Tax line to be displayed. Conditional for models with different screen size.

          Attribute: ans..16

          Valid only when the TransType = SHOWMESSAGE or SHOWITEM.

        • TotalLine

          java.lang.String TotalLine
          Total line to be displayed. Conditional for models with different screen size.

          Attribute: ans..16

          Valid only when the TransType = SHOWMESSAGE or SHOWITEM.

        • ImageDescription

          java.lang.String ImageDescription
          Product name or description, Conditional for models with different screen size and terminal capability.

          Attribute: ans..16

          Valid only when the TransType = SHOWMESSAGE.

        • ItemData

          java.lang.String ItemData
          Support more than one item.

          Attribute: ans..2048

          See Item Data Definition for definition.

          Please note that if the items in one request lager than the capability of screen can display, it only display the latter items. Only valid when TransType = SHOWITEM.

        • EmvKernelConfigurationSelection

          java.lang.String EmvKernelConfigurationSelection
          Please refer to EMV Kernel Configuration Type.

          Attribute: n2

          See EMV-KERNEL-CONFIGURATION-TYPE for definition.

          Please note that this is only valid for EMV contact transaction during authorize card.

          Only valid when TransType = INPUTACCOUNTWITHEMV/AUTHORIZECARD.

        • TransactionDate

          java.lang.String TransactionDate
          9A: Transaction Date Format is YYMMDD.

          Attribute: n6

          TransactionDate Use terminal date by default
          Only valid when TransType = INPUTACCOUNTWITHEMV/AUTHORIZECARD.

        • TransactionTime

          java.lang.String TransactionTime
          9F21: Transaction Time format is HHMMSS.

          Attribute: n6

          TransactionTime use terminal time by default
          Only valid when TransType = INPUTACCOUNTWITHEMV/AUTHORIZECARD.

        • CurrencyExponent

          java.lang.String CurrencyExponent
          5F36: Transaction Currency Exponent

          Attribute: n2

          CurrencyExponent "02" by default
          Only valid when TransType = INPUTACCOUNTWITHEMV/AUTHORIZECARD.

        • CVVPrompt

          java.lang.String CVVPrompt
          Whether CVV need to be prompted during manual entry, Default as 0;

          Attribute: n1

          CVVPrompt
          0: Do not prompt.
          1: Prompt: Bypass Allowed
          2: Prompt: Bypass Not Allowed. Only valid when TransType = INPUTACCOUNTWITHEMV.

        • ZipCodePrompt

          java.lang.String ZipCodePrompt
          Whether Zip code need to prompted during manual entry, Default as 0:

          Attribute: n1

          0: Do not prompt.
          1: Prompt: Bypass Allowed
          2: Prompt: Bypass Not Allowed. Only valid when TransType = INPUTACCOUNTWITHEMV.

        • FallbackSwipeEntryFlag

          java.lang.String FallbackSwipeEntryFlag
          Whether fallback swipe is allowed after reading EMV card failed. Default as 0.

          Attribute: n1

          0 for not allowed. 1 for allowed.
          Only valid when TransType = INPUTACCOUNTWITHEMV.

        • MerchantCategoryCode

          java.lang.String MerchantCategoryCode
          A Merchant Category Code (MCC) is a four-digit number listed in ISO 18245 for retail financial services. MCC is used to classify the business by the type of goods or services it provides. MCCs are assigned by merchant type (e.g. one for hotels, one for office supply stores, etc.) or by merchant name (e.g. 3000 for United Airlines)

          This field can be set for Contact EMV Chip transactions in the AuthorizeCard command and for Contactless transactions in the InputAccountWithEmv command.

          9F15: Merchant Category Code. ("0001" by default)

          Attribute: n…4

        • SignatureBox

          java.lang.String SignatureBox
          Signature Box

          0 – No signature box; Default Value.
          1 – Terminal Preference.
          2 - Display signature box horizontally.
          3 – Display signature box vertically.

          Attribute: n1

          The signature box should be displayed in the last page before the Button Key display.
          If the signature box is displayed, the three button will be automatically assigned to “Cancel”, “Clear” and “Accept” state.
          Signature Box cannot be used with Input Text.

          Only valid when TransType = SHOWTEXTBOX.

        • Label1

          java.lang.String Label1
          Lines of text, (use all available space)

          Attribute: ans..90

          Only valid when TransType = SHOWDIALOGFORM.

        • Label2

          java.lang.String Label2
          Lines of text, (use all available space)

          Attribute: ans..90


          Only valid when TransType = SHOWDIALOGFORM.

        • Label3

          java.lang.String Label3
          Lines of text, (use all available space)

          Attribute: ans..90


          Only valid when TransType = SHOWDIALOGFORM.

        • Label4

          java.lang.String Label4
          Lines of text, (use all available space)

          Attribute: ans..90


          Only valid when TransType = SHOWDIALOGFORM.

        • Label1Property

          java.lang.String Label1Property
          0 - Unchecked (default)
          1 - Checked Relevant only if the ButtonType is “CheckBox”.

          Attribute: n1


          Only valid when TransType = SHOWDIALOGFORM.

        • Label2Property

          java.lang.String Label2Property
          0 - Unchecked (default) 1 - Checked Relevant only if the ButtonType is “CheckBox”.

          Attribute: n1


          Only valid when TransType = SHOWDIALOGFORM.

        • Label3Property

          java.lang.String Label3Property
          0 - Unchecked (default) 1 - Checked Relevant only if the ButtonType is “CheckBox”.

          Attribute: n1


          Only valid when TransType = SHOWDIALOGFORM.

        • Label4Property

          java.lang.String Label4Property
          0 - Unchecked (default) 1 - Checked Relevant only if the ButtonType is “CheckBox”.

          Attribute: n1


          Only valid when TransType = SHOWDIALOGFORM.

        • ButtonType

          java.lang.String ButtonType
          Button Type value:

          Attribute: n1

          1 - Radio Button (default). It allows the user to choose only one of a predefined set of mutually exclusive options, and the API returns right after selection.
          2 - CheckBox. It allows the user to choose multiple entities of a predefined set of options, and the API is returned upon key press. “CANCEL” – API return abort “CLEAR” will clear the CheckBox selection “ACCEPT” – API return accept

          Only valid when TransType = SHOWDIALOGFORM.

        • PaddingChar

          java.lang.String PaddingChar
          For INPUTACCOUNTWITHEMV:
          This is used to indicate what value need to pad on data string before encryption. The default padding char is 0. It is only valid when the Data Encryption Flag is not 0 (no encryption) or 4 (Voltage E2EE). For MACCALCULATION:
          This is used to indicate what value had been padded on data when encryption. Terminal will remove the padding chars first when calculate MAC.
          0: padding 0x00;
          F: padding 0xFF;
          If this field is empty, terminal won’t remove the padding chars.

          Attribute: n1

          Only valid when TransType = INPUTACCOUNTWITHEMV/MACCALCULATION.

        • TrackDataSentinel

          java.lang.String TrackDataSentinel
          This is used to indicate the response track 1 and track 2 data whether need to include start and end sentinels for magnetic swipe transaction.

          Attribute: n1

          0: needn’t to include start and end sentinel.
          1: need to include start and end sentinel.
          The default value is 1.

          Only valid when TransType = INPUTACCOUNTWITHEMV.

        • CameraScanReader

          java.lang.String CameraScanReader
          0- Rear camera (default)
          1 – Front camera

          Attribute: n1

          Only valid when TransType = CAMERASCAN.

        • VASProgram

          java.lang.String VASProgram
          VASProgram:

          1 - Apple Pay VAS
          2 – Google Smart Tap

          Attribute: n1

          Only valid when TransType = VASSETMERCHANTPARAMETERS/VASPUSHDATA.

        • VASMode

          java.lang.String VASMode
          VASMode:

          0 - VAS_ONLY
          1 – VAS_AND_PAYMENT
          2 – VAS_OR_PAYMENT
          3 – PAYMENT_ONLY

          Attribute: n1

          Only valid when TransType = VASSETMERCHANTPARAMETERS/VASPUSHDATA.

        • GoogleSmartTapData

          ManageRequest.GoogleSmartTap GoogleSmartTapData
          Google Smart Tap specific data.
          Only valid when TransType = VASSETMERCHANTPARAMETERS.
          Please refer to ManageRequest.GoogleSmartTapfor detail

        • ApplePayVASData

          ManageRequest.ApplePayVAS ApplePayVASData
          Apple Pay VAS specific data.
          Only valid when TransType = VASSETMERCHANTPARAMETERS.
          Please refer to ManageRequest.ApplePayVAS for detail

        • GoogleSmartTapPushServiceData

          ManageRequest.GoogleSmartTapPushService GoogleSmartTapPushServiceData
          F VAS Push Data.
          Only valid when TransType = VASPUSHDATA.
          Please refer to ManageRequest.GoogleSmartTapPushService for detail.

        • TokenCommand

          java.lang.String TokenCommand
          Token Command.

          1 – Lookup. Query host for card information using token.
          2 – Delete. Delete token from token vault.
          3 – Duplicate/Renew. Generate duplicate token value.
          4 – Update. Update the value of the token or update information.
          Default as 1.

          Attribute: n1

          Only valid when TransType = TOKENADMINISTRATIVE.

        • Token

          java.lang.String Token
          Token value.

          Attribute: ans...128

          Only valid when TransType = TOKENADMINISTRATIVE.

        • TokenSN

          java.lang.String TokenSN
          The merchant’s token serial number. Valid when Token Command is 3.

          Attribute: ans...32

          Only valid when TransType = TOKENADMINISTRATIVE.

        • ExpiryDate

          java.lang.String ExpiryDate
          The token’s expiration date. Format MMYY.
          Valid when Token Command is 4.

          Attribute: n...4

          Only valid when TransType = TOKENADMINISTRATIVE.

        • ContinuousScreen

          java.lang.String ContinuousScreen
          ‘0’ default.
          ‘1’ after command is executed display doesn't go back to idle screen.

          Attribute: n1

        • M1Command

          java.lang.String M1Command
          M1 Command type:

          0: read
          1: write
          2: operate with increase value
          3: operate with decrease value
          4 : operate with backup value
          5: read the serial number

          Attribute: n1

          Only valid when TransType = MIFARECARD.

        • BlockNo

          java.lang.String BlockNo
          Block number indicator

          Attribute: n…2

          Only valid when TransType = MIFARECARD.

        • Password

          java.lang.String Password
          Password to authority. The field is mandatory when M1 Command is ‘0’, ‘1’, ‘2’, ‘3’, and ‘4’.

          Attribute: ans..32

          Only valid when TransType = MIFARECARD.

        • PasswordType

          java.lang.String PasswordType
          Password type:

          0: password A type
          1: password B type
          The field is mandatory when M1 Command is ‘0’, ‘1’, ‘2’, ‘3’, and ‘4’.

          Attribute: n1

          Only valid when TransType = MIFARECARD.

        • BlockValue

          java.lang.String BlockValue
          Only valid when M1 Command type is

          1: write. The format is HEX and the length must be 32. For example, "01234567890123ABCDEF" means "\x01\x23\x45\x67\x89\x01\x23\xAB\xCD\xEF"
          2: operate with increase value. The format is decimal and the max length is 9.
          3: operate with decrease value. The format is decimal and the max length is 9.

          Attribute: ans…32

          Only valid when TransType = MIFARECARD.

        • UpdateBlockNo

          java.lang.String UpdateBlockNo
          Specifies number of block to which operation result will write.
          There are always two purses in M1 card: main purse and backup purse, in order to resume when abnormal error occurs in purse operation. Operation result of main purse is always written to backup purse

          Attribute: n…2

          Only valid when TransType = MIFARECARD.

        • TransactionSequenceNumber

          java.lang.String TransactionSequenceNumber
          Transaction Sequence Number.
          Counter maintained by the terminal that is incremented by one for each transaction

          Attribute: n...8

          Only valid when TransType = INPUTACCOUNTWITHEMV/AUTHORIZECARD.

        • LineItemAction

          java.lang.String LineItemAction
          Line item action:

          0: add(default value)
          1: update
          2: delete

          Attribute: n1

          Only valid when TransType = SHOWITEM.

        • ItemIndex

          java.lang.String ItemIndex
          Use to reference to a line when added. A line can be updated or deleted using this index as reference.
          Multiple lines can be deleted at once using a single command. Each index is separated with a vertical bar ("|") as separator. For example: "1|2|5" (This will affect lines with index 1, 2 and 5).
          If an Item Index is specified when the first item is added, Indexed Mode will be activated. In Indexed Mode, each time an item is added, an Item Index must be provided otherwise an error will be returned. This allows lines to be updated or deleted.
          If an Item Index is not specified when the first item is added, Non-Indexed Mode will be activated. In Non-Indexed Mode, Item Index will be ignored. Lines cannot be updated or deleted.

          Attribute: ans..256

          Only valid when TransType = SHOWITEM.

        • PinpadType

          java.lang.String PinpadType
          Pinpad Type:

          For AUTHORIZECARD/GETPINBLOCK:
          0: Internal Pinpad.(default)
          1: External Pinpad (A80 only, if use other device will return param error).
          2: RNIB Pinpad mode.
          3: External Pinpad first. If external pinpad is present, use external pinpad. If not, use internal pinpad. (A80 only, if use other device will return param error).

          For SHOWTHANKYOU/SHOWMESSAGECENTER/REMOVECARD:
          0: Internal Pinpad. (default).
          1: External Pinpad. (A80 only, if use other device will return param error).
          3: External Pinpad first. If external pinpad is present, use external pinpad. If not, use internal pinpad. (A80 only, if use other device will return param error).

          Attribute: n1

          Only valid when TransType = AUTHORIZECARD/GETPINBLOCK/REMOVECARD/SHOWTHANKYOU/SHOWMESSAGECENTER.
          Since:
          V1.02.01

        • SourceKeyType

          java.lang.String SourceKeyType
          Support Key Type:

          TMK(Terminal Master Key) = 1;
          Note: Now only supports TMK.

          Attribute: n1

          Only valid when TransType = SESSIONKEYINJECTION
          Since:
          V1.03.00

        • SourceKeyIndex

          java.lang.String SourceKeyIndex
          Source Key Index:

          If source key type = TMK, the source key index should be [1,99].

          Attribute: n...2

          Only valid when TransType = SESSIONKEYINJECTION
          Since:
          V1.03.00

        • DestinationKeyType

          java.lang.String DestinationKeyType
          Support Key Type:

          TPK(Terminal PIN Key) = 0;
          TAK(Terminal MAC Key) = 1;
          TDK(Terminal DES Key) = 2;

          Attribute: n1

          Only valid when TransType = SESSIONKEYINJECTION
          Since:
          V1.03.00

        • DestinationKeyValue

          java.lang.String DestinationKeyValue
          Destination key plaintext or the cipher test, for DES keys, the destination key is the ciphertext which encrypted by the source key in the mode of 3DES ECB.
          Valid the length of key value is 8, 16, 24.
          Note: key data must be in ASCII format. For example, the 8 bytes binary array “\x11\x22\x33\x44\x55\x66\x77\x88” can be converted into “1122334455667788”.

          Attribute: ans..48

          Only valid when TransType = SESSIONKEYINJECTION
          Since:
          V1.03.00

        • DestinationKeyIndex

          java.lang.String DestinationKeyIndex
          The destination key index should be [1, 99].

          Attribute: n...2

          Only valid when TransType = SESSIONKEYINJECTION
          Since:
          V1.03.00

        • CheckMode

          java.lang.String CheckMode
          Check Mode:

          0: No KCV check
          1: Perform DES/TDES encryption on 8 bytes 0x00, and use first 4 bytes as KCV.

          Attribute: n1

          Only valid when TransType = SESSIONKEYINJECTION
          Since:
          V1.03.00

        • CheckBuffer

          java.lang.String CheckBuffer
          If check mode = 0, PED won’t check KCV, this data is no meaning.
          If check mode = 1, the KCV length should be 4 bytes.

          Note: the data must be in ASCII format. For example, the 4 bytes binary array “\x11\x22\x33\x44” can be converted into “11223344”.

          Attribute: n1

          Only valid when TransType = SESSIONKEYINJECTION
          Since:
          V1.03.00

        • InputData

          java.lang.String InputData
          The data used for MAC Calculation.

          The data can be divided into several parts by vertical bar (‘|’). Use ‘Encryption Bitmap’ to indicate which parts is encrypted.
          Terminal will remove the vertical bars (‘|’) and decrypt the ciphertext, then MAC will be calculated using the decrypted data.
          The data is represented in hexadecimal. For example: 3031|4a4b|6061.

          Attribute: ans...4000

          Only valid when TransType = MACCALCULATION
          Since:
          V1.03.00

        • EncryptionBitmap

          java.lang.String EncryptionBitmap
          Indicate which parts of input data is encrypted.

          0: Plaintext
          1: Ciphertext
          For example: if input data contain 5 parts. 00101 means the third and the fifth part is ciphertext.
          Default is 0, means this part is plaintext.

          Attribute: var

          Only valid when TransType = MACCALCULATION
          Since:
          V1.03.00

        • MACKeySlot

          java.lang.String MACKeySlot
          Key index in PED: range is [1,99].

          Attribute: n...3

          Only valid when TransType = MACCALCULATION
          Since:
          V1.03.00

        • MACWorkMode

          java.lang.String MACWorkMode
          MAC calculation mode:

          Default is 0.
          0: ANSI X9.9.
          1: Hypercom Fast Mode.
          2: ANSI X9.19.

          Attribute: var

          Only valid when TransType = MACCALCULATION
          Since:
          V1.03.00

        • EncryptionKeySlot

          java.lang.String EncryptionKeySlot
          The key slot used to encrypt input data parts. The key can’t be DUKPT key.

          Attribute: n...3

          Only valid when TransType = MACCALCULATION
          Since:
          V1.03.00

        • FallbackInsertEntryFlag

          java.lang.String FallbackInsertEntryFlag
          Whether fallback insert is allowed after reading contactless card failed. Default as 0.

          0 for not allowed.
          1 for allowed.

          Attribute: n1

          Only valid when TransType = INPUTACCOUNTWITHEMV
          Since:
          V1.03.00

        • AcceptedCardType

          java.lang.String AcceptedCardType
          The card type bitmap, refer to Card Type Bitmap definition.
          It makes POS system available to control which card types accepted for current command. and it is mandatory for fleet transaction.

          Attribute: n32

          Only valid when TransType = INPUTACCOUNTWITHEMV / INPUTACCOUNT

        • AmountLine

          java.lang.String AmountLine
          The Amount information between the TenderType TransType line and “Please Insert/Swipe Card” line on the Screen.

          Attribute: ans...64

          Only valid when TransType = INPUTACCOUNTWITHEMV / INPUTACCOUNT

        • MultiMerchant

          MultiMerchant MultiMerchant
          MultiMerchant Information. see MultiMerchant for detail. Only valid when TransType = GETVAR / SETVAR
          Since:
          V1.04.00

        • KeyType

          java.lang.String KeyType
          The Key Type.

          Attribute: n1

          For GETPEDINFORMATION:
          1 = Master Key
          2 = Session Key
          3 = DES DUKPT Key
          4 = AES DUKPT Key
          Empty value means all key information.If you want to specify Key Slot, you need to specify key type first.

          For INCREASEKSN:
          3 = DES DUKPT Key 4 = AES DUKPT Key

          Only valid when TransType = GETPEDINFORMATION/INCREASEKSN

          Since:
          V1.05.00

        • BarcodeType

          java.lang.String BarcodeType
          The barcode type

          Attribute: n1

          It can be empty or 7 (QRCODE)

          Only valid while transType is SHOWTEXTBOX

          Since:
          V1.05.01

        • BarcodeData

          java.lang.String BarcodeData
          The data length scope is [1, 126]

          Attribute: var

          Only valid while transType is SHOWTEXTBOX

          Since:
          V1.05.01

        • FileType

          java.lang.String FileType
          The file type indicates the file usage.

          Attribute: n1

          The value is as below:
          0: Resource file (Default). A zip package contains multiple files. Terminal will replace the files according to the file names.
          1: Offline RKI key file. A zip package contains the file used to inject offline RKI key.
          2: Installation Package. Update the OS and App through the installation package.
          3: License file. The license file is used to activate the app.
          4: Signed Host Root CA Cert file. Either a signed .pem file or a zip package containing unsigned .pem file and it’s signed files (_S1, _S2, and _S3). The .pem file contains the Host Root CA certificates used for online processing.
          Only valid while transType is UPDATEIMAGE/UPDATERESOURCE.
          Since:
          V1.05.01

        • FilePath

          java.lang.String FilePath
          The path of ".zip" file,

          Attribute: var

          when the file type is 0(ManageRequest.FileType), only image files which meet requirement can affect. Here is some terminal information: MT30 suggested image size is 480*272(width*height in pixels) and format should be 24bit bmp.
          S300 suggested image size is 240*248 and format should be 24bit bmp.
          S500 suggested image size is 320*200 and format should be 24bit bmp.
          S920 suggested image size is 240*280 and format should be png.
          SP30 suggested image size is 320*200 and format should be 24bit bmp.
          PX5/PX7 suggested image size is 800*440 and format should be png.
          A30 suggested image size is 1280*720 and format should be png.
          A60 suggested image size is 720*1280 and format should be png.
          A6650 suggested image size is 1080*2408 and format should be png.
          A77 suggested image size is 720*1440 and format should be png.
          A80 suggested image size is 480*800 and format should be png.
          A910S suggested image size is 720*1280 and format should be png.
          A920 suggested image size is 720*1280 and format should be png.
          A920 Pro suggested image size is 720*1440 and format should be png.
          A920 MAX suggested image size is 720*1480 and format should be png.
          A930 suggested image size is 720*1280 and format should be png.
          Aries6 suggested image size is 1280*720 and format should be png.
          Aries8 suggested image size is 800*1280 and format should be png.
          D190 suggested image size is 320*216 and format should be png.
          D210/D200/SP30s suggested image size is 320*200 and format should be 24bit bmp.
          D220 suggested image size is 480*760 and format should be png.
          IM20 suggested image size is 480*272 and format should be png.
          IM30 suggested image size is 720*1280 and format should be png.
          IM300 suggested image size is 320*216 and format should be png.
          Q20 suggested image size is 480*272 and format should be png.
          Q25 suggested image size is 320*216 and format should be png.
          Q30 suggested image size is 800*440 and format should be png.
          Q80 suggested image size is 480*726 and format should be png.
          M8 suggested image size is 800*1280 and format should be png.
          IM25 suggested image size is 320*480 and format should be png.
          A8700 suggested image size is 800*1280 and format should be png.
          A8500 suggested image size is 720*1280 and format should be png.
          POS expands image if size is below suggested size.

          note:
          1. The Prolin idle image file’s name must be “mt30_ad0.r” /”mt30_ad1.r”/”mt30_ad2.r”.
          2. The Android idle image file’s name must be “mt30_ad0.png” /”mt30_ad1.png”/”mt30_ad2.png” (Except the extern Android device).
          3. The extern Android device idle image file's name must be "exdev_idle.png".

          Only valid while transType is UPDATEIMAGE/UPDATERESOURCE

          Since:
          V1.05.01

        • ReceiptPrint

          java.lang.String ReceiptPrint
          Receipt printing for current command, default as "3 – Both merchant /customer copy":
          0 – no receipt
          1 - merchant only
          2 - customer only
          3 - Both merchant /customer copy

          Attribute: n1

          Only valid while transType is REPRINT

          Since:
          V1.05.01

        • TransactionCVMLimit

          java.lang.String TransactionCVMLimit
          Configure the limit value of CVM for current transaction. (Only for Visa payWave)

          Attribute: n...9

          Only valid while transType is INPUTACCOUNTWITHEMV

          Since:
          V1.07.00

        • MACKeyType

          java.lang.String MACKeyType
          The type of the key which is used to calculate MAC value.

          Default is 0(TAK)
          0: TAK
          1: DUKPT key

          Attribute: n...3

          Only valid when TransType = MACCALCULATION
          Since:
          V1.06.00

        • KSNFlag

          java.lang.String KSNFlag
          Indicate whether to increase KSN, when MAC key type is 1(DUKPT Key)

          For MACCALCULATION:
          Default KSN flag is 0(Not increase)
          0: Not increase.
          1: Increase.

          For INPUTACCOUNTWITHEMV/AUTHORIZECARD/GETPINBLOCK:
          Default KSN flag is 1(Increase)
          0: Not increase.
          1: Increase.

          Attribute: n1

          Only valid when TransType = MACCALCULATION/INPUTACCOUNTWITHEMV/AUTHORIZECARD/GETPINBLOCK
          Since:
          V1.06.00

        • EnableLuhnCheck

          java.lang.String EnableLuhnCheck
          Deprecated. 
          Used to perform luhn algorithm check on the input PAN from manual and swipe entry.

          0 Default. Disable Luhn checking(manual entry only)
          1 Enable Luhn checking(manual entry only)

          Attribute: n1

          Only valid when TransType = INPUTACCOUNTWITHEMV
          Since:
          V1.08.00

        • CustomData

          java.util.List<E> CustomData

          Custom data string to be encrypted, Used to define any special formats for sensitive data (PAN, Track2, etc).


          Custom data must be in hexadecimal format. Placeholder strings should not be converted to hex.


          Placeholder format:
          ${PARAMETER NAME}
          ${VARIABLE NAME, FORMAT,PADLEN}


          PARAMETER NAME – the data which needs encryption. Optional values:
          TRACK1DATA, TRACK2DATA, TRACK3DATA


          VARIABLE NAME – Variable name, optional values:
          TRACK1LEN, TRACK2LEN, TRACK3LEN


          FORMAT – The format of the data, optional values: HEX.


          PADLEN – The number of padding bytes.


          Ex1:
          Original String = “pq${TRACK2DATA}abc”
          Convert custom string to hex: “7071${TRACK2DATA}616263”


          Ex2: Multiple Strings
          [“7071${TRACK2DATA}", "${TRACK1LEN, HEX, 4}616263”]


          Attribute: Var

          Since:
          V1.09.01

        • FallbackToManualEntryFlag

          java.lang.String FallbackToManualEntryFlag
          Whether fallback to manual is allowed after reading swipe card failed. Magnetic Swipe Entry Flag must be enabled.

          Default as 0.
          0 for not allowed.
          1 for allowed.

          Attribute: n1

          Only valid when TransType = INPUTACCOUNTWITHEMV
          Since:
          V1.12.00

        • InputTextTitle

          java.lang.String InputTextTitle
          The title that to be displayed, default as empty.
          Must contain a value if using Input Text.

          Attribute: ans…36

          Only valid while transType is SHOWTEXTBOX
          Since:
          V1.10.00

        • InputText

          java.lang.String InputText
          Input Text:

          0 - No Input Text(default value)
          1 – Show Input Text

          If the input text is displayed, the three button will be automatically assigned to “Cancel”, “Clear” and “Accept” state.
          Input Text cannot be used with Signature Box.
          Input Text needs an Input Text Title value to be used.

          Attribute: n1

          Only valid while transType is SHOWTEXTBOX
          Since:
          V1.10.00

        • IconName

          java.lang.String IconName
          Icon Type:

          ""(empty): No Icon displayed (Default)
          pic_smc1: Display’s the icon titled pic_smc1 in the terminal
          pic_smc2: Display’s the icon titled pic_smc2 in the terminal
          Please Note:
          1. Uploading Show Message Icon Images:
          This can be done using the UPDATERESOURCE command or using TMS. In TMS the parameter will be in the MISC tab in the Image Display Settings section under the “Show Message Center Icon “1” or “2”.
          2.The icon image file name must be renamed to “pic_smc1” or” pic_smc2” to upload the icon image to TMS or UpdateResource.
          3.The icon image’s file extension must be .r or .png when uploading it in TMS.
          4.When uploading the Icon Image with UpdateResource the file extension will vary based on the terminal.
          1)Android terminals support .png files.
          2)Traditional terminals support .r files.
          5. Icon will only display when CustomUI is disabled.
          Please see Terminal Image Formats, Icon Image column for the image’s original format required.

          Attribute: ans..9

          Only valid while transType is SHOWTHANKYOU/SHOWMESSAGECENTER
          Since:
          V1.13.00

        • TargetDevice

          java.lang.String TargetDevice
          Used to update the firmware of the specified device.
          0: Terminal. (Default)
          1: External Pin pad.

          Attribute: n1

          Only valid while transType is UPDATERESOURCE
          Since:
          V1.11.00

        • CustomMACInformation

          ManageRequest.CustomMACInformation CustomMACInformation
          Request the Custom MAC Information.

          Attribute: Var

          Only valid when TransType = INPUTACCOUNTWITHEMV.
          Please refer to ManageRequest.CustomMACInformation for detail
          Since:
          V1.12.00

        • DataEncryptionType

          java.lang.String DataEncryptionType
          The Data Encryption Type.
          0: DES/TDES(Master Session)

          Attribute: n1

          Since:
          V1.14.00

        • WorkMode

          java.lang.String WorkMode
          Key Calculation Work Mode.
          Default is 0.
          If Type is DES/TDES:
          0: DES/TDES ECB Encryption. (Default)
          1: DES/TDES CBC Encryption

          Attribute: n1

          Since:
          V1.14.00

        • KeyIndex

          java.lang.String KeyIndex
          Key Index of key in Ped:
          If Type is DES/TDES, it means TDK index, the range is [1,99].

          Attribute: n..3

          Since:
          V1.14.00

        • UserData

          java.lang.String UserData
          The Data needs Encryption or Decryption:
          The length is less than 1024 bytes and hex string is 2048. The data should be divisible by 8.

          Attribute: ans..2048

          Since:
          V1.14.00

        • InitVector

          java.lang.String InitVector
          Initial vector for CBC mode:
